title: Framework-level governance supplement to DR10 SEAL revision v2 (accepts 78cd22e structurally; documents Option F selection, raw-turnover-only-screen weakening caveat, and future-framework-revision governance rule; binds only to NEW SEAL turns from s14+ onward; existing sealed candidates remain governed by DR10 v1; S13-D1 REJECT_FAST remains terminal)
report_kind: Framework-level governance supplement: accepts DR10 v2 as the binding definition for future s14+ candidates, while documenting the strategic intent, the Option F selection (OR -> AND connective change), the raw-turnover-only-screen weakening caveat versus Options C and B from the master reconciliation memo, the acceptance reasons, and a new future-framework-revision governance rule. Does NOT modify DR10 v2. Does NOT roll back 78cd22e. Does NOT author or revive any candidate. Does NOT select T1/T6/halt path.

acceptance_of_dr10_v2.structural_validity_attestation: DR10 v2 SEAL revision at 78cd22e was independently audited (read-only) and confirmed: seal self-verifies under LESSON_HUNTER_004 canonical recipe; existing sealed candidates byte-stable (s7-D1, s9, s10-D1, s10-D2, s11-D1-v1, s12-D1, s13-D1 all chain artifacts, B005, B006, T8 lineage); old DR10 v1 definition preserved verbatim; new DR10 v2 definition recorded verbatim; binding scope explicitly future-only; ~60 hard boundaries attested; old-vs-new verdict table present as informational only.

s13_d1_terminal_preservation.outcome_under_v1: REJECT_FAST (DR10 v1 fired; turnover branch fired: 84.79 > 0.50; cost_drag branch did NOT fire: 2.35% < 5%; OR-disjunctive => DR10 fires)

strategic_intent_statement.intent: The research intent is to discover retail-scale tradable strategies while preserving fail-closed discipline. DR10 v2 is accepted as a pragmatic recalibration for future candidates, not as a retroactive rescue of any prior candidate and not as permission to ignore cost/turnover risk.
strategic_intent_statement.intent_source: Strategic-intent question surfaced in master reconciliation memo section 10 (commit 1e51680). Operator typed the intent statement verbatim in the authorization phrase for this governance supplement.
what_intent_does_NOT_authorize: any relaxation of the 6 live-block gates
promotion of any existing terminal candidate to OOS/Strategy Lab/live
any candidate authoring in this supplement
selection of T1, T6, or halt path in this supplement
removal of K9 inviolacy (>= 100 closed trades)
removal of K9-reachability or DR10-reachability disciplines
modification of any C1-C8 safety contract

recalibration_framing.kind: pragmatic recalibration of one rule's logical connective
recalibration_framing.what_recalibration_addresses: The structural incompatibility between K9 inviolate>=100 closed trades and DR10 v1 OR-disjunctive turnover threshold for active-trading strategies at retail-scale start_cash on liquid futures (per master memo section 7 and investigation plan sections 4-5). At MNQ retail-scale start_cash (~$10k-$50k), K9 minimum trade count drives annual_turnover above 0.50 even for cost-disciplined strategies. Under v1 OR, such strategies REJECT_FAST regardless of true cost drag. Under v2 AND, such strategies remain admissible if cost_drag stays below 5%.
what_recalibration_does_NOT_address: DR10-reachability discipline (re-framed under v2 AND but still binding)
Other DR rules DR1, DR2, DR3, DR4, DR5, DR6, DR7, DR9 (unchanged)
K-gates K1, K2, K4, K7, K8, K9, K12 (unchanged)
Any prior sealed candidate verdict (all preserved byte-equivalent under v1)

governance_caveat_option_f_weakens_raw_t.caveat: Option F (OR -> AND) removes the ability of DR10 to fire on raw annual_turnover alone. A hypothetical future strategy with very high annual_turnover but cost_drag below the 5% threshold would pass DR10 v2 cleanly, even though it would have REJECT_FAST under DR10 v1. This is a deliberate weakening of the raw-turnover-only screen and is the principal integrity-trade-off documented in master reconciliation memo section 8.
governance_caveat_option_f_weakens_raw_t.partial_compensation_via_DR5: DR5 (cost-stress tier-flip across S0/S1/S2/S3 cost tiers) provides partial overlap: it catches strategies whose edge collapses as costs increase. But DR5 is not identical to a raw-turnover screen and does not fire on the raw-turnover branch alone.
governance_caveat_option_f_weakens_raw_t.calibration_dependency: DR10 v2's effectiveness depends entirely on the cost_drag 5% threshold being correctly calibrated. If real-world cost calibration is too lenient, DR10 v2 becomes a paper tiger. K-gates, C1-C8 contracts, and other DR rules remain available as additional screens.

option_C_master_memo_recommended.integrity_assessment: MORE-INTEGRITY-PRESERVING: honestly drops the turnover proxy rather than gating it behind an AND
option_C_master_memo_recommended.why_not_selected_here: Option F was authored and committed at 78cd22e prior to operator-side master reconciliation memo. Rolling back v2 to adopt Option C would require an additional framework SEAL revision turn (heavyweight) and risks setting a precedent that parallel-authored framework rules can be reversed.

option_B_alternative.definition: annual_turnover > X (recalibrated) OR S2_cost_drag > 0.05 -> REJECT_FAST; X anchored to defensible academic CTA norm (suggested 5-20x annual turnover range)
option_B_alternative.integrity_assessment: PRESERVES BOTH BRANCHES, recalibrates the turnover branch only
option_B_alternative.s13_d1_under_B_hypothetical: DOES_NOT_FIRE on turnover branch if X >= 85 (s13-d1 was 84.79); DOES_NOT_FIRE on cost branch (2.35% < 5%); => DOES_NOT_FIRE overall (if X chosen high enough to admit s13-d1); REJECT_FAST otherwise
option_B_alternative.future_high_turnover_low_cost_strategy_u: REJECT_FAST if turnover > X; ADMISSIBLE if turnover <= X
option_B_alternative.why_not_selected_here: Option B requires defending a specific recalibrated threshold X, opening calibration debate. Option F preserves the original 0.50 thresholds verbatim, reducing calibration scope. Master memo did not recommend B as primary.

reasons: Thresholds unchanged: annual_turnover > 0.50 and S2_cost_drag > 0.05 preserved verbatim. No calibration debate opened.

Cost remains load-bearing: under v2 AND, S2_cost_drag > 0.05 is a required condition for DR10 to fire. The original DR10 name 'turnover_COST_explosion' identifies cost as the primary concern; v2 makes that explicit.
Existing verdicts preserved: all prior sealed candidates including S13-D1 remain governed by DR10 v1 and their terminal verdicts are byte-equivalent and immutable under v2.

Future candidates still require cost-stress testing: DR5 cost-stress tier-flip, S2/S3 cost-tier evaluations, DA4 capital-scaling, and K-gates K1/K2/K4/K7/K8/K9/K12 all remain binding. DR10 v2 is one of nine DR rules and one of several cost-related screens.

No live-readiness implication: live remains BLOCKED_AT_6_GATES; FRC remains NEVER_GRANTED. v2 binds only PLAN/SEAL-time admissibility of new s14+ candidates and has zero effect on the live-readiness gates.
trade_offs_acknowledged: Option F is more permissive than Options C and B for the high-turnover/low-cost cell of the strategy space.
Option F's raw-turnover-only-screen weakening depends on the cost_drag 5% threshold being correctly calibrated to catch high-frequency strategies whose costs are paper-low.
Master reconciliation memo's primary recommendation (Option C) was not adopted; this is documented and accepted in this supplement.

future_framework_governance_rule.rule_text: Future framework SEAL revisions that change any reject-fast condition (any DR rule's definition, threshold, connective, or rejection class) require BOTH: (a) explicit operator-typed authorization phrase in the operator's own controller session, AND (b) a governance supplement (analogous to this one) documenting strategic intent, the specific option selected versus alternatives considered, integrity-preservation ranking, and acceptance reasons. The governance supplement may be authored in the same turn as the framework revision or in a follow-up turn, but it must exist before subsequent framework changes are made.
applies_to: all DR rules (DR1-DR9 and any future DR rule)
all K-gates (K1, K2, K4, K7, K8, K9, K12 and any future K-gate)
any new reject-fast condition introduced at framework level
does_not_apply_to: candidate-record-id-scoped PLAN/SEAL authoring (governed by existing per-candidate authorization workflow)
L1 carry-forward supplements (governed by existing per-chain-step L1 supplement pattern)
lifecycle memos for terminal candidates (governed by existing P11 PARK / P7 decision memo workflow)
read-only audits (governed by existing read-only authorization)
future_framework_governance_rule.rule_documentation_anchor: This supplement file (framework_dr10_v2_governance_supplement.json) is the canonical reference for this rule.

this_supplement_does_NOT: Authorize any new candidate or candidate_record_id.
Authorize the T1 RSI MNQ next-research-track from 30c836e.
Authorize the T6 next-research-track alternative from 30c836e.
Authorize the halt path from the post-s13-d1 next-track selection plan.
Authorize revival of any parked candidate (s7-D1, s9, s10-D1, s10-D2, s11-D1-v1, s12-D1, s13-D1, B005, B006, T8 lineage).
Authorize promotion of s13-D1 to OOS, P10, Strategy Lab, live, or FRC.
Authorize promotion of any other terminal candidate beyond its sealed verdict.
Modify DR10 v2 thresholds, connective, or precedence chain position.
Roll back 78cd22e or any of its boundary attestations.
Author or commit a competing framework SEAL revision.
Author a new candidate Tier-N PLAN or DRAFT.
Modify any sealed candidate artifact.
Modify any existing candidate artifact in any way.
Run backtests, diagnostics, simulations, or any Strategy Lab invocation.
Fetch market data from Databento, Yahoo, yfinance, broker, or exchange.
Modify lessons.md or any brain_memory project file.
Modify tmp/ helper scripts.
Modify app.py or any Research Orchestrator file.
Modify any Command Center file.
Modify the runner harness, strategy code, or cache.
Modify CLAUDE.md, .gitignore, or any safety contract.
Stage or commit anything.
Grant FRC.
Relax any of the 6 live-block gates.
Relax K9 inviolacy (>= 100 closed trades).
Relax K9-reachability or DR10-reachability disciplines.
Make any profitability claim, live-readiness claim, or OOS-confirmation claim.
negative_invariants: This supplement does not constitute a backtest result.
This supplement does not constitute an OOS validation.
This supplement does not constitute a live-readiness attestation.
This supplement does not grant FRC.
This supplement does not unlock any of the 6 live-block gates.
This supplement does not relax K9.
This supplement does not modify DR10 v2 at 78cd22e in any way.
This supplement does not author any candidate.
This supplement does not select any next-research-track.
This supplement does not commit anything (memo-only).
audit_trail_disclaimer: This supplement is INFORMATIONAL AND GOVERNANCE ONLY. It does not modify the DR10 v2 SEAL at 78cd22e, does not change any candidate's terminal verdict, and does not authorize any new candidate, T1/T6, live activity, FRC grant, or live-block-gate relaxation. Operator typed the authorization phrase verbatim in the controller session; this artifact records the operator-typed strategic intent and the governance caveat about Option F's raw-turnover-only-screen weakening relative to the master reconciliation memo's recommendations.
